template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP) Screening

condition.name: Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP)
condition.definition: Vaso-proliferative disorder of the immature retina that can lead to blindness if not detected early.

additional: GA >34 weeks or BW >2000 grams with risk factors
Risk factors: prolonged oxygen therapy, sepsis, multiple transfusions

screening_timing.general_rule: Screen by 30 days of life (earlier if very premature or sick)

preparation: Ensure thermal stability and feeding before procedure
documentation: Presence or absence of plus disease
stop_screening_when: Postmenstrual age ≥45 weeks with no active disease
parent_counseling: Screening is essential even if baby appears normal — बच्चा सामान्य दिखे तब भी आंखों की जांच ज़रूरी है।
Multiple follow-up visits are required — कई बार जांच करानी पड़ेगी।
Procedure is safe — जांच सुरक्षित है।
Early treatment can prevent blindness — जल्दी इलाज से अंधापन रोका जा सकता है।
Do not miss any scheduled eye examination — कोई भी आंख की जांच की तारीख़ न छोड़ें।
Premature babies need long-term eye follow-up — समय से पहले जन्मे बच्चों को लंबे समय तक आंखों की जांच चाहिए।
quick_checklist_for_opd: Refer urgently if treatment threshold reached
flow_logic: If ROP present → assess stage and zone
Continue till full vascularization or PMA 45 weeks

standard_prescription_format.title: ROP Screening Prescription & Follow-up Sheet

type_1_rop: Urgent referral for laser therapy / anti-VEGF

parent_advice: Baby may need multiple eye examinations

warning_signs: White reflex in pupil — आंख की पुतली में सफ़ेद चमक
Abnormal eye movements — आंखों की असामान्य हरकत
Baby not following objects with eyes — बच्चा आंखों से चीज़ों को न देखना
Missed screening appointment — जांच की तारीख़ छूटना
expected_course: ROP screening begins at 2-4 weeks postnatal age depending on gestational age. Most preterm infants will have immature retina that vascularizes normally. Type 2 ROP requires close monitoring. Type 1 ROP requires urgent treatment (laser therapy or anti-VEGF). Screening continues until full retinal vascularization or PMA >=45 weeks without active disease.
key_clinical_points: Screen all infants with GA <=34 weeks or birth weight <=2000 grams.
First screening by 30 days of life (earlier if very premature or sick).
Pupil dilation with Tropicamide 0.5% + Phenylephrine 2.5% before examination.
Document zone, stage, and presence of plus disease at each screening.
Type 1 ROP requires treatment within 48-72 hours of diagnosis.
Stop screening when full vascularization into Zone III or PMA >=45 weeks without active disease.

severity_assessment.mild: No ROP or Stage 1 without plus disease — routine follow-up every 1-2 weeks.
severity_assessment.moderate: Stage 2 or any stage with plus disease features — weekly monitoring, close ophthalmology follow-up.
severity_assessment.severe: Stage 3 or more, Type 1 ROP, aggressive posterior ROP — immediate referral for laser therapy or anti-VEGF treatment.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every 1-2 weeks until retina fully vascularized
